conclusion: The c8 fullnative bug is not at layer3 full-attention input and not an immediate large layer3 output failure. The first over-atol effect appears when layer4 computes linear-attention attn_input from the slightly-drifted layer3 output, then amplifies by layer7 pre-QKV. Candidate fix targets are layer3 full-attention output numerical parity, layer4 RMSNorm sensitivity/precision, or a need to keep rowchunk/per-row semantics through the layer3→4 handoff.

interpretation.layer3_input: exact for fullnative and rowchunk vs native-c1, so the first full-attention layer3 input is not the source.
interpretation.layer3_output: fullnative layer3 output has only small under-atol drift (max_abs 0.000122) and no failed rows; rowchunk exact.
interpretation.layer4_input: after layer3 handoff / layer4 RMSNorm, fullnative layer4 attn_input has first over-atol drift (max_abs 0.0078125 row0) while rowchunk remains exact.
interpretation.layer4_output: drift collapses back to under-atol by layer4 output in the L8/decode1 window, explaining why short final output can remain eq_ok.
interpretation.layer7_preqkv: fullnative over-atol drift expands to all rows by layer7 pre-QKV; rowchunk remains exact.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: focused upstream stage extraction localizes the c8 fullnative drift: layer3 input exact, layer3 output only under-atol drift, first over-atol drift appears at layer4 attn_input after handoff/RMSNorm, and expands by layer7 pre-QKV. This directly advances the hidden/full-attention blocker.
